  • Area Control - Generates a report which shows a list of events where Users or the System have requested to arm or disarm Areas. Optionally filterable by Areas, Users, Arm/Disarm events, User/System events, and Successful/Failed events.
  • User Permissions Summary - Generates a report which shows the list of item permissions and Permission Groups that each User has been assigned. Has the option of generating different levels of detail for reports, depending on how much information is required. Optionally filterable by Users and Items.
  • Item Permissions Summary - Generates a report which shows the list of all Users that have permission to interact with each item. Similar to the User Permissions Summary, except the report is grouped by the item. Optionally filterable by Users and Items.
  • User Current Location - Generates a report which shows a list of Users along with the Area that they are currently in. Optionally filterable by Areas.
  • User Inactivity - Generates a report which shows all Users who have not interacted with the system (i.e. accessed a Door, armed an Area) for a given period of time (default is 1 month).
  • User Details - Generates a report containing detailed summaries of Users in the system. The details shown for each user include (if applicable) the User's Name, Notes, LCD Terminal Profile, Email Address, Assigned Cards & Remote Fobs, and more. Optionally filterable by Users, and Users who are Expired/Expiring Soon.
  • Time On Site (Read-In, Read-Out) - Generates a report which shows a list of time periods that Users have been on site for, based on access events from the specified Read-In/Read-Out Readers. Optionally filterable by Users. This type of report is recommended if the site has strict Badge-in, Badge-out policies when entering and leaving site. Report will show the amount of time between each badge in and each badge out, listing per-user each site visit over the report timeframe. Read-In and Read-Out readers must be specified.
  • Time On Site (First Badge In, Last Badge Out) - Generates a report which shows a list of Users have been on site each day and for how long, based on their first and last reader badge events for the day (a single day is treated as being from 12am-11:59pm). Optionally filterable by Users and Doors. This type of report is recommended if Users are only on site during regular daytime hours (i.e. no night shifts going past midnight), and if your site don't have strictly defined in/out readers.
  • Storage Unit Control - Generates a report which shows a list of events where Users or the System have requested to secure or unlock Storage Units. Optionally filterable by Storage Units, Users, Secure/Unlock events, User/System events, Successful/Failed events, and whether Vacancy Control events should be included.
  • Integration Event History - Generates a report which shows a list of events raised by an Integrated Connection or its Integrated Devices. Optionally filterable by Integrated Devices, as well as specific event types.

Chooses the file format of the report.

  • PDF - Generates the report as a single PDF document. NOTE: This option may be unable to handle creating very large reports (e.g. User Permissions Summary Reports of 500+ pages) due to system limitations; add filters to the data to reduce the report size or choose one of the other file formats if you need to create a very large report.
  • HTML - Generates the report as a single HTML document.
  • Multi-PDF (zipped) - Generates the report as a series of individual PDF documents of approximately 150 pages each at maximum, packed together for downloading in a single ZIP file.
  • CSV - Generates the report as a comma-separated value (CSV) text file, ideal for manipulation or processing in external applications, e.g. spreadsheet software. NOTE: CSV reports are text-only and therefore lack the formatting of the other file type options; if the report's readability is important, consider using one of the PDF or HTML format options instead.

Generate a report which user have requested with Title Text as in Format \"Report Name (Report Type)\".User can provide name for Report in Report Name field.

By default Report Name would be Report Type and this case Title Text would be in \"Report Type\".

The type of timeframe that will be used to fetch events to be included in the generated report.

  • Recent Events - Allows you to specify an amount of time from the present to fetch events to be put into the generated report. For example, picking 2 Weeks as the Timeframe for this option gets the last 2 Weeks of events in the report.
  • Date Range - Allows you to specify a Start and End Date which defines the time period to fetch events from.
Specifies the start date for finding events that will be included in the generated report.
Specifies the end date for finding events that will be included in the generated report.
Specifies how far back in time in time to go when generating the report.
Optionally filter the report results to only include users that are currently in the specified Areas. By default (when the list is empty), all Areas are included in the report.

Specifies whether to exclude Users from the report if the system does not currently know what Area they are in (i.e. inactive users). NOTE: Only applies when the report is not filtered to specific Areas.
Optionally filter the report results to only include the selected Users. By default (when the list is empty), all Users are included in the report.
Optionally filter the report results to only include the selected Items. By default (when the list is empty), all Items (Doors, Areas, Outputs) are included in the report.

Chooses the level of detail to be included in the User Permissions Report.

  • Fully Flattened Permissions (Standard) - The report shows a flattened view of all the items a user has access to through both direct assignment and Permission Groups, and also the conditions which determine when these permissions are available (e.g. Always, only during Working Hours). This type of report is best suited for getting a clear view of all the possible actions that Users can perform in the system.
  • Fully Flattened Permissions + Group Chains (Advanced) - The report contains all of the information in Fully Flattened Permissions (Standard), as well as a description of why these permissions are available to the User (e.g. directly assigned, inherited from a Permission Group). This type of report is best suited for getting a highly detailed, in-depth look at all of the possible actions that Users can perform in the system, as well as the reason(s) why these permissions are available to them.
  • Directly Assigned Permissions only (Basic) - The report shows all of the directly assigned permissions for each user, including the Permission Groups they are assigned to. This report does not flatten out Permission Groups and shows only the direct assignments. This type of report is best suited for getting a simple overview of what items each User has been directly assigned permission to in the system, or seeing which Users have been assigned to which Permission Groups.
Optionally filter the report results to only include the selected Doors or Lift Cars. By default (when the list is empty), all Doors and Lift Cars are included in the report.
Specifies whether to include only Access Granted events, only Access Denied events, or both (show both by default).

Optionally filter the report to only include Users who have expired, or are due to expire soon.

  • All Users - Show all Users regardless of expiry status.
  • Expired Users - Show only Users who have expired.
  • Users Expiring Soon - Show only Users who are due to expire soon, the Expires Within option controls the time period to search.
  • Users Expiring Within - Show only Users who are due to expire within a specified timeframe with a Start Date and End Date.
Specifies how far ahead in time the report should look for Users who are due to expire.
Specifies the start date for finding Users who are due to expire within that date range.
Specifies the end date for finding Users who are due to expire within that date range.
Optionally filter the report results to only include the selected Areas. By default (when the list is empty), all Areas are included in the report.

Optionally filter the report to only include Arm or Disarm events.

  • All Events - Show all Arm/Disarm events.
  • Arm Events Only - Show only Arm events.
  • Disarm Events Only - Show only Disarm events.

Optionally filter the report to only include User or System control events.

  • All Events - Show all User/System events.
  • User Control Events Only - Show only User Control events.
  • System Control Events Only - Show only System Control events.

Optionally filter the report to only include Successful or Failed events.

  • All Events - Show all Successful/Failed events.
  • Successful Arm/Disarm Events Only - Show only successful Arm/Disarm events.
  • Failed Arm/Disarm Events Only - Show only failed Arm/Disarm events.
Select whether events in the Security message category should be included in the report.
Select whether events in the Access message category should be included in the report.
Select whether events in the Hardware message category should be included in the report.
Select whether events in the System message category should be included in the report.
Select whether events in the Audit message category should be included in the report.
Optionally filter the report results to only include the selected Storage Units. By default (when the list is empty), all Storage Units are included in the report.

Optionally filter the report to only include Secure or Unlock events.

  • All Events - Show all Secure/Unlock events.
  • Secure Events Only - Show only Secure events.
  • Unlock Events Only - Show only Unlock events.

Optionally filter the report to only include Successful or Failed events.

  • All Events - Show all Successful/Failed events.
  • Successful Secure/Unlock Events Only - Show only successful secure/unlock events.
  • Failed Secure/Unlock Events Only - Show only failed secure/unlock events.

Optionally include Vacancy events.

Vacancy Cleared events are categorised as Secure events. Marked as Vacant events are categorised as Unlock events.

Selects which readers to use as Read-In Readers in the report. At least one Read-In Reader is required to generate the report. Readers cannot be in the Read-In Readers list if they are also in the Read-Out Readers list.

Selects which readers to use as Read-Out Readers in the report. At least one Read-Out Reader is required to generate the report. Readers cannot be in the Read-Out Readers list if they are also in the Read-In Readers list.
Optionally filter the report results to only include the selected Doors. By default (when the list is empty), all Doors are included in the report.
Specifies whether to include only Violation events, only Forgiveness events, or both (shows both by default).
Optionally filter the report results to only include the selected Integrated Devices. By default (when the list is empty), events from all Integrated Devices are included in the report.

Specifies the type of action that this Scheduled Task should perform when it is executed.

  • Backup Database - Creates a database backup file containing the database contents as they are at the time the scheduled task runs.
  • Create Report - Creates a report with the specified parameters given. Allows creation of any type of report that can be created from the Reports page.

Specifies how the scheduled task should send out the resulting output file after it is produced.

  • Send as Email Attachment - Send the output file to one or more Users via their email address.
  • Copy to Attached USB Drive - Writes the output file to the root folder of an attached USB drive, if one is currently plugged into the Inception Device and accessible.
  • Copy to Network Share - Copies the output file to a Samba network share folder using the provided user/domain credentials.

Specifies the list of Users to send the output file to via email. If there are no users specified, or all specified Users have an invalid or missing email address, the task will fail to send the output file.

Specifies the network share folder to copy the output file to, e.g: //example-server/shared.

NOTE: The folder specified in the network path must already exist in order for the operation to complete successfully.
Specifies the username to use for network/domain authentication. If on a domain network, the domain may also need to be specified before a / character, e.g domain/username.
Specifies the user's password to use for network/domain authentication.

Specifies how often this Scheduled Task should be automatically run.

  • None - This task will not run at regular intervals. Use this option if you plan to execute this task either manually or only when certain conditions occur, e.g. triggering it as part of an Automated Action.
  • Basic - This task will be run at set intervals of uniform length, e.g. Daily, Weekly, Monthly, at a specific time of day.
  • Advanced - Utilises the Calendar items in Inception (configured via the [Configuration > Scheduling > Calendars] page). This task will run on a set of days specified by a separate Calendar item, at a specific time of day. Multiple Calendar/Time of Day combinations can be specified to make the task run at additional times.

Specifies the interval that determines how long to wait in between task executions.

  • Daily - The task will run every day at the specfied Time of Day.
  • Each Week - The task will run every week on the specified Day of Week, at the specfied Time of Day.
  • Every 2 Weeks - The task will run every 2 weeks on the specified Day of Week, at the specfied Time of Day, starting on the closest future applicable day/time at the time the Scheduled Task item is saved.
  • Every 4 Weeks - The task will run every 4 weeks on the specified Day of Week, at the specfied Time of Day, starting on the closest future applicable day/time at the time the Scheduled Task item is saved.

Specifies the day of the week on which this task should be run, in accordance with the rest of the schedule configuration settings.

Not applicable if Daily is chosen as the frequency.
Specifies the time of day on which this task should be run, in accordance with the rest of the schedule configuration settings.

Specifies the Calendar items and times at which the scheduled task should run. The scheduled task will run at the time specified, on the days provided by the calendar.

Each row defines a different schedule entry, for example, you could define 2 Calendar items to represent Weekends and Public Holidays respectively, and set the task to run at 2:00pm on Weekends and 5:00pm on Public Holidays, and the task will run on every day that either of the relevant Calendars are active for, at their respective scheduled time.

Specifies the readers that can control the entry points to this storage block that allows a user to present either their PIN, card, or other credential. Multiple readers can be assigned to the same storage block if required, allowing readers to be mounted in different locations or be of different types.

The selected readers can also be connected to other items such as doors or gates, allowing the User's access request to an entry gate or a building to also be used to control the units within the storage block.

On successful read of a User's credential, the system will unlock any units within the block that the user has Control on Block Access permission to. This allows a User's access to site to automatically unlock their unit.

To add a reader to the storage block, click the Add Items button, this will open a window where you select the readers to add. If there are no readers in this window, new readers can be configured by navigating to the [Configuration > Hardware] page.

Specifies the readers that can control the exit points to this storage block that allows a user to present either their PIN, card, or other credential. Multiple readers can be assigned to the same storage block if required, allowing readers to be mounted in different locations or be of different types.

The selected readers can also be connected to other items such as doors or gates, allowing the User's access request to an exit gate or to exit a building to also be used to control the units within the storage block.

On successful read of a User's credential, the system will secure any units within the block that the user has Control on Block Access permission to. This allows a User's request to exit a building or the site to automatically secure their unit as they leave.

To add a reader to the storage block, click the Add Items button, this will open a window where you select the readers to add. If there are no readers in this window, new readers can be configured by navigating to the [Configuration > Hardware] page.

Allows one or more outputs to be associated with this storage block. If any units in the block are unlocked,secured or in alarm state, the outputs in this list will behave according to output behavior. If output behavior of an output is lighting, this will turn on output when unit is unblocked. If output behavior of an output is Secure Indicator, this will turn on output when unit is secure. If output behavior of an output is Alarm Indicator, this will turn on output when unit is in Alarm state.

For example, these outputs could be used to control lighting, secure indicator and alarm indicator within a section of the building, turning on if any units in that section are unlocked, secure and in alarm state.

To add outputs to the storage block, click the Add Items button, this will open a window where you select the outputs to add. If there are no outputs in this window, new outputs can be configured by navigating to the [Configuration > Hardware] page.

Optionally defines a delay before the Block Outputs are turned off. When set to None, these outputs will turn off immediately. When set to any other value, the outputs will wait this amount of time before turning off.

This is useful for scenarios where the final unit was secured at a keypad inside the facility, and some time is required before the interior lights are turned off.

If configured, units within this storage block will automatically re-secure after a period of time.

This helps ensure that a unit will automatically re-secure after a while and not be left in an unlocked state.

As this option requires knowledge of a unit's door state, the unit must have at least one input with the Door Position Sensor behaviour.

If None is chosen, the resecure logic does not occur.

If a unit is part of more than one block, the shortest time that is not None is chosen for that unit.

If configured, units within this storage block will enter an Unlocked Too Long state if the unit remains unlocked for the specified time. This state is cancelled once the unit is secured or if marked as vacant.

This helps identify if a unit has been unintentionally left unlocked for a long period of time. It also helps identify if users are intentionally unlocking and staying in a unit.

If None is chosen, the resecure logic does not occur.

If a unit is part of more than one block, the shortest time that is not None is chosen for that unit.

This setting determines the response to the sealing of all inputs of a storage unit that is in Alarm.

By leaving this option disabled, the unit will remain in Alarm until a Clear Alarms or Unlock action occurs for the unit, latching the alarm state. Clearing the alarm could be by the units tenant presenting their credential at the Block entry reader to unlock it, or by a User controlling the unit via the [State / Control > Control Storage Units] page on the Inception web interface. This allows users to easily see which unit triggered the alarm, and allows monitoring stations to know when the alarm event has actually been restored.

If the option is enabled, the Unit alarm state will be cleared as soon as all inputs within the secure unit are sealed or when the unit is unlocked. This allows the unit alarm state to follow the state of its inputs, but has the consequence that if a unit only has a door sensor then someone could open a secure unit door, enter, then close it straight away, and the unit would no longer be in alarm.

For unmanned storage unit facilities, it is recommended that this option is disabled and that the area configured to monitor the storage units alarm states has the Cancel Alarm on All Inputs Secure option enabled. This will allow sirens and alarms to occur if someone accesses a secure storage unit, but if it was an accident, then the units tenant can unlock the unit and the alarm can be cancelled.
Outputs with the Lighting behaviour will be turn on when the unit is vacant if Turn on Lighting Outputs When Unit is Vacant is enabled

Allows the units that belong to this block to be selected. Access requests at the Entry Readers and Exit Readers specified above can automatically unlock units within this list, and the Block Outputs will be controlled based on the unit states.

The status of these units are summarized for this block, indicating if all units are secure, or if any are unlocked, unlocked too long, in alarm, or have been tampered with.

To add a unit to the storage block, click the Add Items button, this will open a window where you select the units to add. If there are no units in this window, new units can be configured by navigating to the [Configuration > Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units] page.

Specifies the inputs that are used to monitor this Unit. Each input can have a Behaviour specified, which determines how that input's state affects the Unit.

  • Door Position Sensor - An input with this behaviour lets the system know if the Unit's door is currently opened or closed. In addition, if the Unit is Secure, activation of this input will put the Unit into the Alarm state.
  • Detector - An input with this behaviour will cause a Secure Unit to enter the alarm state when it activates

If an input is tampered with, the unit will enter into a Tamper state.

To add inputs to the Unit, click the Add Items button, this will open a window where you select the inputs to add. If there are no inputs in this window, new inputs can be configured by navigating to the [Configuration > Hardware] page.

Allows one or more outputs to be associated with this Unit. If the unit is Unlocked, the outputs in this list will turn on, and when the Unit is Secure, they will turn off.

For example, these outputs could be used to control a light within the unit or the Unit's door lock. They could also be used as a visual indicator if the unit is Secure (if wired to the Normally Closed contacts of the relay, or if the Invert Output State option is enabled for that output on the [Configuration > Outputs > Hardware Outputs] page).

To add outputs to the Unit, click the Add Items button, this will open a window where you select the outputs to add. If there are no outputs in this window, new outputs can be configured by navigating to the [Configuration > Hardware] page.
Select calendar exceptions to apply to this Time Period.
These exceptions are days/times in which this time period will be inactive. These are defined in the Calendars menu.

Defines the start and ends times of the time period, for each day of the week. During these times and on the days selected the time period will be valid/active.

If the start time is earlier than the end time (e.g 9am -> 5pm), then the time period will be valid during those times on the day specified. Alternatively, if the start and end time are the same (5pm -> 5pm) or the end time is earlier than the start (10pm -> 2am), then the valid block will start on the day specified and end on the next day. This allows an easy way to specify time blocks spanning midnight. This means, for exmaple, 10pm -> 2am on a Wednesday will be valid from 10pm to midnight on Wednesdays, and valid from midnight to 2am on Thursdays. Similarly, if the start and end times match, 5pm -> 5pm on Wednesday will be valid for 24 hours from 5pm Wednesday to 5pm Thursday.

For the time period to be valid for an entire day, 12am -> 12am can be configured, or the All Day (24hr) can be selected.

To add additional time blocks, click the Add Items button, this will open a window where the times and days can be selected.

A list of exceptions which apply on the days when their associated calendar is active (higher priorities will override lower ones). A time period that contains calendar exceptions will have its active or inactive state overridden by the calendar exception on the days for which it’s configured.

Calendar exceptions are used when the same time change for a day should be repeated on multiple days throughout the year. The calendar can be updated, which could affect multiple Time Periods in a consistent way. A common use case example for calendar exceptions are extended trading hour days, where trading hours change consistently on many days throughout the year. The calendar in that case could apply to multiple time periods, like working hours, automatic door times and automatic arm times, each reacting to the same calendar. A user can then update the calendar throughout the year and the system reacts correctly.

Start and end times for the exception are programmed in the same way as the Weekly Recurring Times option above, where a start time that is earlier than the end time (e.g 9am -> 5pm) affects a single day, but the same start and end time (5pm -> 5pm) or an earlier end time (10pm -> 2am) will span midnight and affect the following day too. More details can be seen in the tooltip of the Weekly Recurring Times option above.

To add a calendar exception, click the Add Items button, this will open a window that contains all the available calendars. If there are no calendars, they can be created in the [Configuration > Scheduling > Calendars] menu.

A list of individual time and date based exceptions which override the list of weekly recurring times (higher priorities will override lower ones). Exceptions behave in the same manner as calendar exceptions, overriding the active or inactive state of the time period's weekly recurring times, however these exceptions only exist within this particular time period.

Commonly used to add a temporary specific change to the time period's normal behaviour without changing the recurring times, which could also be added in advance. For example, shutting down for a maintenance period or a brief period of extended trading hours.

To add an additional exception, click the Add Items button, this will add a blank exception.

The security PIN is a unique code that allows a user to control areas and outputs from an LCD terminal, or access a door from a Wiegand keypad reader.

The security PIN can also be used to log onto the web interface instead of using a password. The security PIN will not be visible once the user is saved, if the user has forgotten their security PIN, you may press the “Send PIN” button. This will open a new window which allows an email with the Security PIN and basic details to be sent to the user. Otherwise, another user with higher privileges (such as an installer) with “Reveal PIN” permissions may use this button to reveal and view the PIN.

Security PINs must be unique across users, you will be unable to save changes to a user if a duplicate pin is entered. To generate a unique pin for a user click the Auto-Generate PIN button, this will open a window containing the new PIN. The default requirements for the security PIN length is a minimum of 2 digits and a maximum of 4 digits. If you enter a pin outside of these requirements, you will be unable to save the changes. These PIN length limits can be changed by an administrator by navigating to [Configuration > General > System]. Note, if increasing PIN length limits, LCD Terminals can accept at most 8 digit PINs and the Auto-Generate PIN is limited to at most 10 digits.

A credential is a uniquely numbered card, fob, or other type of identifiable data (e.g. a biometric fingerprint or face scan). The credential number is read by a reader at a door to identify which user is attempting to access the door.

A credential can only be assigned to a single user, if a duplicate credential is assigned you will be unable to save the user. Users may be assigned multiple credentials if, for example, your site uses more than one type of card or biometric reader technology.

To add a credential to a user, click the Add Items button, this will open a window where you select the credential template. If there are no templates in this window, a template can be configured by navigating to [Configuration > Access Control > Credential Templates].

Once a template is selected, enter the number that is printed on the card (or other credential type). If there is no number on the card, you can identify the credential number by navigating to [State / Control > Review Events] and then badging the credential on the reader, find the most recent Credential Read Failed Because Credential is Unassigned event, this event will contain the credential number.

Credentials also contain a state field (which defaults to Active) which determines whether the credential will be accepted at door readers or not. Door readers will only accept credentials marked as Active, credentials marked as Inactive - Lost or Inactive - Stolen for example, will be rejected, and a review event and system message about the inactive credential usage will be logged. All credentials marked as Inactive are handled the same way by the system, the extra information such as Lost or Stolen is provided only for informational purposes. This functionality can be useful to disable the use of a credential in the event that a user's credential is reported as lost/stolen, without having to delete the credential entry from the User's data.

Mobile Credentials are uniquely numbered cards used as identification for this user that is handled by a Mobile Access system. This requires a Mobile Access system to be linked through [Configuration > Access Control > Mobile Access > Mobile Access Connection Settings].

When the system has been linked, the Allocate Mobile Credential button will be usable. This will allocate a Mobile Credential to the user, with an email invitation sent to the specified email. The invitation can be accepted on their phone with the Mobile Access application. That user may only have one Mobile Credential from any given system.

The system uses a preconfigured Mobile Access Credential Template that can be found in [Configuration > Access Control > Credential Templates]. The site code of this template will be the site code specified when linking the Mobile Access system.

A remote fob is a wireless 1 or 4 button keychain remote that is used to remotely control areas, doors, outputs etc. The 1 button remote can also be used as a mobile duress pendant.

A remote fob can only to be assigned to a single user, if a duplicate fob is assigned you will be unable to save the user.

To add a remote fob to a user, click the Add Items button, this will open a window where you select the fob template. The fob template determines the functionality for the buttons on the remote. If there are no templates in this window, a template can be configured by navigating to [Configuration > Access Control > Remote Fob Templates].

Once a template is selected, enter the six-digit serial number that is printed on the remote fob. If there is no number on the remote, you can identify the serial number by navigating to [State / Control > Review Events] and then pressing a button on the remote fob. Find the most recent Remote Fob Button from Unknown Fob event, this event will contain the serial number.

The functionality of the remote assigned to the user is based on the programming of the fob template found in [Configuration > Access Control > Remote Fob Templates].

Permissions determine what areas, doors, outputs and lift floors a user can access or control in the system. The permissions also determine what items the user can control via the web interface.

To add permissions to a user, click the Add Items button; this will open a window that contains all the permissible items. These items can be added one at a time or as a pre-defined permission group.

Permission groups are a collection of permissions that are created in the [Configuration > Users > Permission Groups] page. Permission groups are created to suit a role of one or more users within a business, for example, warehouse staff or admin staff. This allows the same set of permissions to be assigned to multiple users.

The permissions assigned to a user always apply. More advanced control, such as limiting When a user is allowed to do something, is available within permission groups. This allows more complex permissions that fit in with a sites rules to be created, limiting access based on a time period or something else in the system, while keeping the interface used by end-users simpler.

Each permission can be marked as Allow or Deny. A user must be assigned an Allow permission to be allowed to do something. If set to Deny, then the user will be unable to perform that action. Deny permissions override Allow permissions, that is, if someone is allowed through a door due to a permission group, denying access to the door will override this. This can be used when a user should have all of the permissions in a permission group with an exception or two. Those exceptions could then be added as a Deny row.

The LCD terminal profile determines what menus a user can view when logged into an LCD Terminal. The user will require a Security PIN to be able to login to an LCD Terminal. The terminal profile will not affect the logged off functionality of the LCD Terminal such as logged off output control and door control using the OK button.

The profile only determines which menu options the user can view and interact with via the LCD Terminal. If the profile lets the user access the menus that allow area, door or output control, they can see the current state of those items but still need permission to control them in their Permissions below.

Click the dropdown box to select an LCD Terminal Profile. As this is a security and access control system protecting the site, when selecting a profile, it is recommended that you select the one that lets the user do and see everything that they need to, but nothing extra.

The LCD Terminal Profiles are configured on the [Configuration > Users > Terminal Profiles] page.

If enabled, when this user is granted access to a door, the door will unlock for the duration programmed in its Extended Unlock Time, instead of its Default Unlock Time. This option is used for users who require extra time to enter a door, like a delivery driver bringing in parcels or a user with a disability or impaired mobility.

The extended lock time can be configured differently for each door and can be programmed in [Configuration > Access Control > Doors].

If enabled, this user's cards will be permantly stored in the internal card caches of any Standard LAN Access Modules (SLAMs) on the module LAN.

The SLAM is capable of holding 2000 cards in an internal cache, tracking whether access was granted the last time it was presented at a reader and remembering this per-door controlled by the SLAM. If communications between the SLAM and the Inception system are lost, the SLAM can use this card cache to determine if a user's card should be allowed through the door, allowing offline behaviour.

Cards are automatically removed from this cache for several reasons; if access is denied to a door when the card is used, after 1 month of inactivity, or if the 2000 limit is reached then as new cards are added to the cache the oldest cards are removed. This attempts to allow an intelligent offline behaviour where the people who use a door recently will still be able to access the door in the event that the door module loses connectivity with the Inception controller.

This option however can force a card to remain stored in the cache, even if it hasn't been used for a long time. This allows important cards like those belonging to managers, emergency services or fire wardens etc to remain working if something happens to the main controller, even if they are rarely used.

Note that cards are not automatically added to the card cache of each SLAM, the card must be presented at each reader of each door and access be successfully granted for the card to be added to the cache of that module.

If enabled, the user will have access to the web interface using the username and password below. The web interface can be accessed on any device with a supported web browser. The device needs to be on the same local network as the Inception Controller or be connected to the Inception Controllers Wi-Fi Hotspot (requires the optional Inception Wireless Adapter). A user can also remotely connect to the web interface using the Skytunnel service, Skytunnel is a hassle free internet based connection method.

The Enable Web Login option should only be enabled if the user needs to edit programming, control areas, doors, outputs and inputs or to perform administration tasks from the web interface.

You need to assign a web page profile to a user to determine which pages the user can view and interact with. If the chosen web profile allows control of items such as areas, doors or outputs, the user will only be allowed to control the items that you assign in their user permissions. If the user only has permissions to control an area between working hours the same restriction applies to the control of the area from the web interface.

A login name that is used to identify a user and allow access to the web interface. The username can be any length and any combination of letters, numbers and characters.

Each user requires a unique username, you will be unable to save the user programming if a duplicate username is entered.

A secret series of numbers or letters used with the username to allows access to the web interface. The password can be any length and any combination of letters, numbers and characters.

Since Inception is a security product, it is recommended that this password be kept confidential by the user that it has been assigned to. It is also recommended that the password is not saved by the browser that is used to access the web interface. The Inception Controller's web interface can also be accessed over the internet via the Skytunnel architecture, so it is important to have a secure password that can not be guessed.

The Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) enrolment status of the user. 2FA-enrolled users must enter an extra security code from a personal device before they can log into the web interface. Click the link button to open a dialog window to enrol the user for 2FA. NOTE: 2FA must be enabled in Inception's System Settings in order to enrol users for 2FA.

Once a user has been enrolled for 2FA, they will be prompted to enter a security code generated from their personal device in order to confirm the enrolment was successful.

Pressing the X button will delete the existing 2FA configuration for the user. This will cause the user's existing 2FA accounts on smartphone apps to be rendered obsolete, and will require the user to re-enrol for Inception 2FA in order to use it again.

Once a user has initially enrolled for 2FA, the enrolment dialog will no longer be accessible by clicking the link button for security reasons, the 2FA configuration will have to be deleted and re-created in order to re-enrol.

Determines which web pages this user is allowed to see within the web interface and how they are allowed to interact with them; for example view only, modify and control.

Click the dropdown box to select a web page profile. As this is a security and access control system protecting the site, when selecting a profile, it is recommended that you select the one that lets the user do and see everything that they need to, but nothing extra.

Web profiles can be configured on the [Configuration > Users > Web Page Profiles] page.

The IR Connect account that is linked to this user. Click the link button to enter the user's IR Connect account (email address). If they don't have an IR Connect account, an email will be automatically sent instructing them how to set up their free account.

Linking an IR Connect account to this user allows the user to control Areas, Doors and Outputs remotely using the IR Connect smartphone app. The app is free to use and only requires that the Inception controller has an active internet connection. Only the items this user has permission to control will be seen in the App. Pressing the X button will unlink this user from the IR Connect account, preventing them from logging in.

The IR Connect account also allows the user to monitor the system remotely by receiving push notification alerts to their smartphone. The notifications sent are configured on the [Configuration > System > Notifiers] page.

Note: To enable push notifications to the user’s smartphone, an App subscription is required, please contact your security installer for more information on setting this up.

The IR Connect app can be downloaded from the Apple App store and Google Play store.

The API Token which is linked to this user, which can be used in the authorization header of REST API requests to make requests on the behalf of this user instead of logging in with another request.

If an API Token is created through the prompt on the link button, it can be copied and added to the authorization header of REST API requests (with type 'APIToken' preceding it).

Unlinking the API Token through the 'X' button will result in the API Token generated for the user being deleted and being rendered unusable.

The user Photo field is an optional field where an image file can be uploaded and associated to a user.

User photos will be displayed with their name in the Who column on the [Events] page when events involving this user occur in the system, e.g. Area Arm/Disarm or Door Access events. Users without photos will only have their name displayed in the Who column.

An image file can be assigned to a user by dragging and dropping the file directly onto the image upload area (if using a PC), or by clicking the upload area and selecting the image that you want to upload. The image's dimensions will be scaled down to 480 x 640px (3:4 aspect ratio) if they exceed this size, so it is recommended that uploaded images meet these conditions for optimal quality when uploaded. The image can be cropped using the Edit button, or deleted with the Remove button.

Images can be assigned in bulk to users by using the Import User Photos button to upload a ZIP file with multiple image files for different users. For more information, click the Import User Photos button to open the import dialog.

The email address that is used when sending email notifications to this user.

This option can be left empty if email notifications for this user are not used. Otherwise, the address must be in a valid format such as jsmith@example.com

If enabled, this user will not be allowed to access the system before the User Start Time if one is provided, or after the User Expiry Time if one is provided. If a user is restricted from accessing the system, disabling this option or changing the start or expiry time will re-enable the user again.

User expiry is commonly used for contractors or other users who require access to the system for a limited period of time. It allows their user to be configured in advance with credentials and permissions, but only activate at a future time. Similarly, the user can be automatically restricted to prevent their access at a date and time in the future, removing the need for a user to remember to manually edit or delete the user in the future.

Once expired, this user will not be able to interact with the system in any way, preventing access to the web interface and the LCD terminal, credentials will no longer work at readers, their RF fob will not work and they will no longer receive notifications.

Determines the date and time when this user will become valid. If set in the future, this user will not be allowed to access the system. This option will only apply when the Enable User Expiry option above is enabled. If blank, no start time will be used.

See the tooltip for the enable option above for more information about user expiry.

Determines the date and time when this user will expire. This option will only apply when the Enable User Expiry option above is enabled. If blank, no expiry will be used.

See the tooltip for the enable option above for more information about user expiry.

If configured, the user will be cancelled when they present their credential to the system for the first time. Depending on configuration, this could be their card or PIN.

This feature is often used where single-use credentials are given to visitors to access the site, which prevents unlimited access in case the card is stolen. The above user expiry options could also be used to manage visitor cards, allowing access for the day only. This option instead allows allows multiple cards to be pre-created (one per user) and handed out as required, without needing to log into the Inception system and update the expiry time for that day.

Once a credential has been used and the user cancelled, the User Cancelled checkbox below will be automatically ticked. To reset the user and allow the credential to be used again, un-tick the checkbox and save the user.

If enabled, this user will be restricted from accessing the system in any way, similar to user expiry above. This option is automatically enabled when the above When to Cancel User option is used.

The user can be reset by un-ticking this option and saving the user.
If enable, mobile credentials of this user will be revoked upon passing User Expiry time.By default, this option will be unchecked.
Add permissions to this user. Permissions can be added to specific doors, areas or outputs or entire permission groups can be added.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Alarm Reporting] page.

  • None - The page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Alarm Reporting] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them. The user will be able to send a manual test report, but not be able to download the Contact ID Report
  • Modify - The [Alarm Reporting] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited. The user will be able to download the Contact ID Report

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Edit Areas], [Area Creation], [Area Input Map] and [Area Test] pages.

  • None - The [Edit Areas], [Area Creation], [Area Input Map] and [Area Test] pages will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Edit Areas] and [Area Test] pages can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them. The [Area Creation] and [Area Input Map] pages will not be accessible.
  • Modify - The [Edit Areas] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited. The [Area Creation], [Area Input Map] and [Area Test] pages can all be viewed and settings can be edited.

Note: A user's ability to control areas on the [Edit Areas] page is determined by their user permissions and the Area State option in the Item Monitoring Permissions section of their web page profile.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Automated Actions] page.

  • None - The page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Automated Actions] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Automated Actions] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Calendar] page.

  • None - The [Calendar] page will not be accessible. The user will still be able to add and remove Calendar Exceptions on the [Time Periods] page.
  • View - The [Calendar] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Calendar] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Note: Users are often allowed to modify calendars as, for example, one could be configured for Extended Trading Hour Days, which would update various time periods in the system

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Credential Templates] page.

  • None - The [Credential Templates] page will not be accessible. The user will still be able to select a credential template on the [Manage User] page when assigning a card to a user.
  • View - The [Credential Templates] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Credential Templates] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Remote Fob Templates] page.

  • None - The [Remote Fob Templates] page will not be accessible. The user will still be able to select a remote fob template on the [Manage User] page when assigning a remote fob to a user.
  • View - The [Remote Fob Templates] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Remote Fob Templates] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Connections] page.

  • None - The [Connections] page will not be accessible. The user will still be able to select a connection when configuring automated actions or remote fob templates.
  • View - The [Connections] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Connections] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Counters] page.

  • None - The [Counters] page will not be accessible. The user will still be able to select a counter when configuring automated actions.
  • View - The [Counters] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Counters] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Custom Outputs] and [Set As User Controllable] pages. A custom output is a non physical output used in Automated Actions.

  • None - The [Custom Outputs] and [Set As User Controllable] pages will not be accessible. Custom outputs can still be used in trigger conditions and action responses on the [Automated Actions] page.
  • View - The [Custom Outputs] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them. The [Set As User Controllable] page also depends on the Edit Hardware Outputs option. If it is set to View or Modify the page can be viewed, but no editing is allowed.
  • Modify - The [Custom Outputs] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited. The [Set As User Controllable] page also depends on the Edit Hardware Outputs option. If it is set to View the page can be viewed, if set to Modify it can be edited.

Note: A user’s ability to control custom outputs on the [Custom Outputs] page requires that:

  • The custom output configured as User Controllable.
  • The custom output assigned to the user’s Permissions
  • The Output State option is set to Control in the Item Monitoring Permissions section of their Web Page Profile.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Custom Inputs] page. A custom input is a non physical input used in Automated Actions.

  • None - The [Custom Inputs] page will not be accessible. Custom inputs can still be used in trigger conditions and action responses on the [Automated Actions] page.
  • View - The [Custom Inputs] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Custom Inputs] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Note: A user’s ability to control an input on the [Hardware Inputs] page requires that:

  • The Input State option is set to Control in the Item Monitoring Permissions section of their Web Page Profile.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Date and Time] page.

  • None - The [Date and Time] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Date and Time] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them. The Set Time Manually option is also unavailable on the [Date and Time] page.
  • Modify - The [Date and Time] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited. The Set Time Manually option will be available in the toolbar on the [Date and Time] page.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Edit Doors] page.

  • None - The [Edit Doors] page will not be accessible.
  • View - [Edit Doors] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Edit Doors] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Note: A user’s ability to control a door on the [Edit Doors] page requires that:

  • The door is assigned to the user’s Permissions
  • The Door State option is set to Control in the Item Monitoring Permissions section of their Web Page Profile.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Hardware] page.

  • None - The [Hardware] page will not be accessible.
  • View - [Hardware] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them. The Configure, Remove, Refresh and Create New icons will be removed from the toolbar on the [Hardware] page.
  • Modify - The [Hardware] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited. The Configure, Remove, Refresh and Create New icons will be visible on the toolbar of the [Hardware] page.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Hardware Outputs] and [Set As User Controllable] pages.

  • None - The [Hardware Outputs] and [Set As User Controllable] pages will not be accessible. Hardware outputs can still be selected in all sections of programming such as Siren Outputs or Strobe Outputs on the [Edit Areas] page.
  • View - The [Hardware Outputs] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them. The [Set As User Controllable] page also depends on the Edit Custom Outputs option. If it is set to * View or Modify the page can be viewed, but no editing is allowed.
  • Modify - The [Hardware Outputs] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited. The [Set As User Controllable] page also depends on the Edit Custom Outputs option. If it is set to View the page can be viewed, if set to Modify it can be edited.

Note: A user’s ability to control hardware outputs on the [Hardware Outputs] page requires that:

  • The hardware output configured as User Controllable.
  • The hardware output assigned to the user’s Permissions
  • The Output State option is set to Control in the Item Monitoring Permissions section of their Web Page Profile.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Hardware Inputs] page.

  • None - The [Hardware Inputs] page will not be accessible. Inputs can still be selected in all sections of programming such as the [Edit Areas] and [Area Input Map] pages.
  • View - The [Hardware Inputs] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Hardware Inputs] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Note: A user’s ability to control an input on the [Hardware Inputs] page requires that:

  • The Input State option is set to Control in the Item Monitoring Permissions section of their Web Page Profile.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Access Control > Lift/Elevator > Lift Car] page.

  • None - The [Access Control > Lift/Elevator > Lift Car] page will not be accessible. Lift cars can still be selected in all sections of programming if required.
  • View - The [Access Control > Lift/Elevator > Lift Car] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Access Control > Lift/Elevator > Lift Car] page can be viewed, settings can be edited and lift cars can be created or deleted.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Access Control > Lift/Elevator > Lift Floor] page.

  • None - The [Access Control > Lift/Elevator > Lift Floor] page will not be accessible. Lift Floors can still be selected in all sections of programming such as user permissions.
  • View - The [Access Control > Lift/Elevator > Lift Floor] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Access Control > Lift/Elevator > Lift Floor] page can be viewed, settings can be edited and lift floors can be created or deleted.

Note: A user’s ability to control a lift floor on the [Access Control > Lift/Elevator > Lift Floor] page requires that:

  • The Lift Floor State option is set to Control in the Item Monitoring Permissions section of their Web Page Profile.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units] page.

  • None - The [Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units] page will not be accessible. Storage Units can still be selected in all sections of programming such as user permissions.
  • View - The [Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units] page can be viewed, settings can be edited and storage units can be created or deleted.

Note: A user’s ability to control a storage unit on the [Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units] page requires that:

  • The Storage Unit State option is set to Control in the Item Monitoring Permissions section of their Web Page Profile.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Blocks] page.

  • None - The [Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Blocks] page will not be accessible. Storage blocks can still be selected in all sections of programming.
  • View - The [Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Blocks] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Blocks] page can be viewed, settings can be edited and storage blocks can be created or deleted.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Network] page.

  • None - The [Network] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Network] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Network] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Notifications] page.

  • None - The [Notifications] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Notifications] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Notifications] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Permission Groups] page.

  • None - The [Permission Groups] page will not be accessible. Permission groups can still be selected in sections of programming such as the [Manage Users] page.
  • View - [Permission Groups] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Permission Groups] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Readers] page.

  • None - The [Readers] page will not be accessible. All of the reader settings can still be changed in the Inception Controller or Standard LAN Access Module (SLAM) setup on the [Configuration > Hardware] page.
  • View - [Readers] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Readers] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Scheduler] page.

  • None - The [Scheduler] page will not be accessible.
  • View - [Scheduler] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Scheduler] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Note: A user with permission to the [Scheduler] page will be able edit area and door schedules, without needing permission to modify doors or areas. This lets them update those settings without being able to change everything else about doors and areas.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Scheduled Tasks] page.

  • None - The [Scheduled Tasks] page will not be accessible.
  • View - [Scheduled Tasks] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Scheduled Tasks] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [System] page.

  • None - The [System] page will not be accessible.
  • View - [System] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [System] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Terminal Profile] page.

  • None - The [Terminal Profile] page will not be accessible. Terminal Profiles can still be assigned to users in the [Manage Users] page.
  • View - [Terminal Profile] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Terminal Profile] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Time Periods] page.

  • None - The [Time Periods] page will not be accessible. Time Periods can still be selected in all sections of programming such as the [Manage Users], [Scheduler] and [Automated Action] pages.
  • View - [Time Periods] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Time Periods] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Manage Users] page.

  • None - The [Manage Users] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Manage Users] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Manage Users] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

If enabled, allows a user to import and export users via CSV. These options are available in the toolbox of the [Manage Users] page.

This option can only be enabled if the user has Modify permissions to the Edit Users option above.

If enabled, allows a user to reveal other user's PINs as long as they have a lower or same priority level. Consider if allowing this user to reveal PINs of lower or same priority level users is appropriate, and if any others should even be allowed to do it (the Installer user has this permission by default). Revealing a user's PIN can be done by pressing the 'Send PIN' button present on the user's management page.

If a user forgets their PIN, they do not require this permission, and can instead either generate a new PIN or use the 'Send PIN' (accessible to anyone) option to send their forgotten PIN to their email address (if they have one).

This option can only be enabled if the user has Modify permissions to the Edit Users option above.

If enabled, allows a user to reveal stored passwords of various entities that have a Password field, such as IR Video Gateway Connections, providing they have the Modify permission to this entity. Revealing a password can be done by pressing the button present on the entity's password field.

If enabled, users with this profile option will only be allowed to assign permission groups to other users on the [Manage Users] page and will have the individual areas, doors, outputs and so on hidden. By hiding these, the user management process is simplified for end users.

This allows the detailed site permissions to be configured by a technician or a system administrator, while a user responsible for day to day management has a simpler interface. Also, if a site has complex permissions (i.e permission is only allowed during working hours, or advanced door interlocking scenarios etc), it prevents a user from assigning permission to the item directly and circumventing the site rules.

The above Limit User Permissions Config to Groups Only option must be enabled to use this filter.

If this list is empty, this user will be able to use any permission group while managing other user's permissions. Adding items to this list restricts the user to only being able to use the specified items when editing permissions.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Web Page Profiles] page.

  • None - The [Web Page Profiles] page will not be accessible. Web Page Profiles can still be assigned to users in the [Manage Users] page.
  • View - The [Web Page Profiles] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the current settings but not modify them.
  • Modify - The [Web Page Profiles] page can be viewed and the settings can be edited.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Control Areas] page, as well as their ability to control areas from other pages.

  • None - The [Control Areas] page will not be accessible. The area state will still be displayed on the [Edit Areas] page and the Favourite Items Section of the [Dashboard] page, but no control is allowed
  • View - The [Control Areas] page can be viewed, allowing a live view of area state to be shown. The areas still cannot be controlled from any page.
  • Control - The [Control Areas] page can be viewed and the areas can be controlled. The areas can also be controlled from the [Edit Areas] page and the Favourite Items section of the [Dashboard] page.

Note: A user’s ability to control an area requires that:

  • The area is assigned to the user’s Permissions.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Control Doors] page, as well as their ability to control doors from other pages.

  • None - The [Control Doors] page will not be accessible. The door state will still be displayed on the [Access Control > Doors] page and the Favourite Items Section of the [Dashboard] page, but no control is allowed.
  • View - The [Control Doors] page can be viewed, allowing a live view of door state to be shown. The door still cannot be controlled from any page.
  • Control - The [Control Doors] page can be viewed and the doors can be controlled. The doors can also be controlled from the [Access Control > Doors] page and the Favourite Items section of the [Dashboard] page.

Note: A user’s ability to control an area requires that:

  • The door is assigned to the user’s Permissions.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Control Inputs] page, as well as their ability to control inputs from other pages.

  • None - The [Control Inputs] page will not be accessible. The input state will still be displayed on the [Configuration > Inputs] page, but no control is allowed
  • View - The [Control Inputs] page can be viewed, allowing a live view of input state to be shown. The inputs still cannot be controlled from any page.
  • Control - The [Control Inputs] page can be viewed and the inputs can be controlled. The inputs can also be controlled from the [Configuration > Inputs] page.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [Control > Outputs] menu.

  • Deny - The [Control > Outputs] menu will not be accessible from any LCD Terminal.
  • Allow - The [Control > Outputs] menu can be viewed, allowing the user to control outputs.

Note: A user’s ability to control an output from an LCD Terminal requires that:

  • The output is configured as User Controllable on the [Configuration > Outputs > Set As User Controllable] page and the output is assigned to the user’s Permissions.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Control Lift Floors] page, as well as their ability to control lift floors from other pages. Control of lift floors allows a user to set floors to free access, letting users access those floors without needing to present a credential.

  • None - The [Control Lift Floors] page will not be accessible. The floor state will still be displayed on the [Configuration > Access Control > Lift Floor] page, but no control is allowed.
  • View - The [Control Lift Floors] page can be viewed, allowing a live view of lift floor states to be shown. The lift floor still cannot be controlled from any page.
  • Control - The [Control Lift Floors] page can be viewed and the lift floors can be controlled. The lift floors can also be controlled from the [Configuration > Access Control > Lift Floors] page.

Note: A user’s ability to control a lift floor requires that:

  • The lift floor is assigned to the user’s Permissions with the Control permission.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Control Storage Units] page, as well as their ability to control storage units from other pages. Control of storage units allows a user to unlock a unit, letting the unit be opened and accessed without an alarm. It also allows setting a unit to Vacant, preventing alarms or being marked as unlocked too long, or automatically resecuring after a period of time.

  • None - The [Control Storage Units] page will not be accessible. The unit state will still be displayed on the [Configuration > Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units] page, but no control is allowed.
  • View - The [Control Storage Units] page can be viewed, allowing a live view of storage unit states to be shown. The storage unit still cannot be controlled from any page.
  • Control - The [Control Storage Units] page can be viewed and the storage units can be controlled. The storage units can also be controlled from the [Configuration > Access Control > Storage Unit > Storage Units] page.

Note: A user’s ability to control a storage unit requires that:

  • The storage unit is assigned to the user’s Permissions with the Control or Storage Unit Vacancy permissions. Note that Vacancy Control permission can only be granted via Permission Groups.

Determines a user's ability to interact with the [Control Integrated Devices] page, as well as their ability to manually control integrated devices on that page. Control of integrated devices allows a user to send manual commands to the integrated device.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [Area Walk Test] page.

  • None - The [Area Walk Test] page will not be accessible.
  • Control - The [Area Walk Test] page can be viewed, allowing the user to view the state of currently active walk tests, as well as begin and cancel them.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [Hardware Test] page.

  • None - The [Hardware Test] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Hardware Test] page can be viewed, allowing the user to view the state of and control inputs and outputs for each module.

Note: This option should only be enabled for the installer as it allows a user to control outputs that are not configured as User Controllable or assigned to their permissions.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [Commissioning Checklist] page.

  • None - The [Commissioning Checklist] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Commissioning Checklist] page and the Commissioning Checklist Header Notification can be viewed.

Note: When assigning a web page profile the ability to view the [Commissioning Checklist] page, configure it to interact with all the pages contained within the [Commissioning Checklist] page.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [Review Events] page.

  • None - The [Review Events] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Review Events] page can be viewed, allowing the user to view a historical log of system events.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [Control Counters] page.

  • None - The [Control Counters] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [Control Counters] page can be viewed, allowing the user to view the state of counters.
  • Control - The [Control Counters] page can be viewed and the counters can be controlled, allowing counter values to be modified if required.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [View Hardware] page.

  • None - The [View Hardware] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [View Hardware] page can be viewed, allowing the user to view the state of hardware modules.
  • Control - The [View Hardware] page can be viewed and the hardware modules can be controlled, allowing hardware to be isolated if required.

Note: Control permission should only be enabled for the installer or system administrators, as isolation of hardware causes health issues to be ignored by the system.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [View Peripherals] page.

  • None - The [View Peripherals] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [View Peripherals] page can be viewed, allowing the user to see the state of peripherals attached to hardware modules.

Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Control Scheduled Tasks] page, as well as their ability to control tasks from other pages.

  • None - The [Control Scheduled Tasks] page will not be accessible. The tasks state will still be displayed on the [Configuration > Scheduled Tasks] page, but no control is allowed.
  • View - The [Control Scheduled Tasks] page can be viewed, allowing a live view of task state to be shown. The tasks still cannot be controlled from any page.
  • Control - The [Control Scheduled Tasks] page can be viewed and the tasks can be controlled. The tasks can also be controlled from the [Configuration > Scheduled Tasks] page.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [System Overview] page.

  • None - The [System Overview] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [System Overview] page and both the System Warnings and System Messages header notifications can be viewed, allowing the user to see the overall state of the Inception system.

System messages are informational, telling the user about events that happened in the past such as a system reset or a health issue that has been restored, and just need to be acknowledged to remove. System warnings highlight anything currently within the system that may compromise it's performance, allowing the user to either fix the issue themself or inform someone who has the ability to fix the problem.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [View Time Periods] page.

  • None - The [View Time Periods] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [View Time Periods] can be viewed, allowing the user to see the state of a time period and the Next Time Period Change.

Determines a user’s ability to view the [View Users] page.

  • None - The [View Users] page will not be accessible.
  • View - The [View Users] can be viewed, allowing the user to see current location of all users.
  • Control - The [View Users] page can be viewed and the users state can be controlled, allowing anti-passback violations to be forgiven.

If enabled, when a user visits the [Control Areas], [Control Doors], [Control Outputs] or [Control Lift Floors] page, they will only be able to see items that they have permission to control. Often enabled for end user profiles, providing them with a simpler list of items to see.
If enabled, the user can generate reports about the system from the [System > Reports] page.
If enabled, the user can backup the database from the [System > Backup/Restore] page.

If enabled, the user can restore the database from the [System > Backup/Restore] page.

Caution: Permission to execute this task should only be provided to a site administrator or the installer. The user would be able to restore an old backup disabling recently programmed functionality.

If enabled, the user can reset the controller back to the factory default from the [System > Backup/Restore] page.

Caution: Permission to execute this task should only be provided to the installer. A user with this permission can completely reset the controller disabling all programmed functionality.

If enabled, the user can update the firmware of the Inception Controller, LAN Modules or peripherals from the [System > Update Controller] or [System > Update Module] pages.

Caution: Permission to execute this task should only be provided to the installer. Updating of firmware may include additional tasks that need to be followed which, if not performed correctly, can compromise site security.

If enabled, the user can upload custom items provided by Inner Range from the [Configuration > System] page.

Note: The user also requires at least View permission on the Edit System Settings option in their web page profile to be able to see the [Configuration > System] page.

If enabled, the user can place the system into service mode, which will disable alarm reporting and siren responses.

Caution: Permission to execute this task should only be provided to a site administrator or the installer. A user with this permission can compromise site security.
Determines a user’s ability to interact with the Cross Reference feature on item editor pages.

A profile's priority level is used to create a user management hierarchy, where users are only allowed to create, edit or delete users who have a profile priority level that is lower or equal to their own. Priority levels range from one to five, with a higher number translating to a higher priority.

This options provides a more secure way of protecting the system, preventing for example the person who is assigned the day to day user management from being able to create a new Installer or Administrator user. Similarly, a more senior user with more permissions and abilities can't be edited by a user with a lower priority level.

Web Page Profiles with Priority Five will be able to edit any Web Page Profile's permissions.
Determine's a user's ability to view the API Details page under [System].
Determines a user’s ability to interact with the [Licensing] page.

If enabled, the [Dashboard] page will shows shortcuts to items an installer will generally use, including the commissioning checklist, hardware wizard, alarm reporting and credential templates.

Note: If the user’s web profile does not include the ability to view these items, the respective shortcuts will not appear on the [Dashboard] page.

If enabled, the [Dashboard] page will shows shortcuts to items a system administrator will generally use, including networking, date and time, system overview and database backup/restore.

Note: If the user’s web profile does not include the ability to view these items, the respective shortcuts will not appear on the [Dashboard] page.

If enabled, the [Dashboard] page will show shortcuts to items used commonly in day-to-day use, including editing users, permission groups, automated schedules and calendars.

Note: If the user’s web profile does not include the ability to view these items, the respective shortcuts will not appear on the [Dashboard] page.

If enabled, the [Dashboard] page will show shortcuts to guided tours that will guide the user through various tasks in the system.

Note: The specific guided tours will not be displayed if the user does not have permission to the task the tour is demonstrating.

If enabled, the [Dashboard] page will show shortcuts to view/control doors, areas, outputs, inputs and events.

Note: If the user’s web profile does not include the ability to view or control one of these items, the respective shortcuts will not appear on the [Dashboard] page.
